conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
Sorry to hear that. Can you tell me a bit about what you've been up to lately? Any new projects or classes at school?
Yeah, I'm trying to finish my degree, but it's been tough with all the stuff going on. Classes are fine, I guess. It's just...I don't know, everything feels like such an effort.
You're feeling a bit exhausted? What does that look like for you in your daily life?
I mean, it's not just exhaustion. It's more like my brain feels foggy all the time. Even simple tasks take forever.
That sounds really frustrating. Have you noticed any changes in your social life or relationships lately?
Honestly? Yeah, pretty much everyone I know is going through their own stuff, so it's hard to even muster up the energy for small talk.
I understand that can be tough. How are you sleeping these days?
Sleep's been a real struggle. Sometimes I'll fall asleep at 10 PM and wake up at 2 AM feeling like I didn't sleep at all. And then I'm just left feeling...done.
That sounds incredibly challenging. What about your appetite? Has that changed at all for you?
Appetite's been shot. Sometimes I'll skip meals altogether, 'cause the thought of eating seems too overwhelming.
It sounds like you're really struggling with motivation and energy. Are there any hobbies or activities that bring you joy anymore?
Honestly? Not really. I used to love hiking, but even that feels like too much effort now.
That makes sense. Can you tell me more about what's going on at school? What are some of the challenges you're facing in your classes?
It's just...I'm not focusing well enough, and my grades are slipping. I feel like I'm falling behind, and it's really stressing me out.
So, it sounds like you're experiencing some pretty significant concentration issues. Can you give me an example of what that looks like in your daily life?
Like, I'll be trying to study for a test, but my mind will just keep wandering off. I'll miss important details or forget what I was supposed to do.
That can be really frustrating. What about your physical sensations? Are you experiencing any body feelings that might be related to this?
Honestly? Yeah, sometimes I get these weird tension headaches that feel like they're going to last forever. And my neck and shoulders are always sore from poor posture.
That sounds uncomfortable. Have there been any recent stressful life events or changes that might be contributing to your symptoms?
Yeah...my best friend moved away for a job, so I don't have as much social support anymore. And my parents are going through some marital issues, which is really taking a toll on me.
That can be tough to navigate. What kind of coping strategies do you typically use when dealing with stress and anxiety?
Um...I guess just trying to power through and push my feelings aside? I don't know, it's not like I have any other tools in my toolbox.
Okay, that makes sense. What are some things that you think would be important for us to work on together during our sessions?
Honestly? Just getting my energy back and being able to focus again. That sounds stupid, but...
Not at all! It's a great place to start. So, what do you hope to achieve in our next session?
I guess just maybe feeling a little more motivated and less overwhelmed. Baby steps, right?
Absolutely. We can take it one step at a time. Is there anything specific that's been causing you the most distress or discomfort lately?
Honestly? Just the feeling of being stuck in this rut and not knowing how to get out of it.
I understand. It sounds like you're really struggling with feelings of powerlessness. Can we explore some strategies for building your confidence and motivation?
Yeah, that would be amazing. Thanks for listening.
You're welcome. We'll work on that together in our next session. Is there anything else you'd like to add or discuss before we wrap up today?
No...I think that's it for now. Thanks again.
You're welcome. Take care of yourself, and I'll see you at our next appointment.
